purpose: A compact categorical label used to tag items by category, priority, or type. Non-semantic (no state colors) — purely for classification. Common in inbox lists, message centers, and search results.
whenToUse: Categorize list items by type (e.g. 'Invoice', 'Purchase Order', 'RFP')
Show priority levels without state colors ('P1', 'P2', 'P3')
Tag items with tenant, department, or region
Group similar items visually in a compact form
whenNotToUse: For a KPI with a value — use GenericTag
For a full state indicator — use Tag with color
As a clickable filter — use Token or FilterItem
doRules: Use consistent colors within a category set (all 'Invoice' labels same color)
Place InfoLabel before the primary text in a list item
Use the neutral palette by default; colored variant only when categories need distinction
dontRules: Do not use InfoLabel for state or status — that's ObjectStatus's job
Do not stack multiple InfoLabels on one item — combine or use fewer
Do not put values inside InfoLabel — that's GenericTag
Do not use as a link — InfoLabel is display-only

layoutGuidance.placement: Inline at the start of a list item or table cell

requirements: Label text has 4.5:1 contrast against InfoLabel background
Color coding is decorative — semantic meaning always in text
